searchText: CABINETS/DATACOM SX SERIES SERVER CABINETS/DATACOM DATACENTER SERVER CABINET,SX SERIES.pdf Data Centre Server Cabinet,Sx Series Al Jaddaf, Dubai, United Arab Emirates, P.O. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com DATACENTER SERVER CABINET,SX SERIES APPLICATION STANDARDS COMPLIANCE FEATURES • Datacenter Applications • Server/Storage Applications • Security system Applications • Co Location in Datacenter • Multi tenent Datacenter • ANSI/EIA RS- 310-D,IEC 297-2 • DIN 41491 PART 1,DIN 41497 PART 7 • GB/T3047.2-92,EIA 310E • ROHS & REACH • 71 % perforated Doors. • Vertically Split double wing doors. • Loading Capacity up to 1500 Kg. • Maximum Passive Ventilation. • Handle locks are located on the doors. • Standard rear doors with spring hinges enables ease of use. • 2 Vertical Cable Managers. • Made in Turkey DESCRIPTION Datacom SX series are modular Cabinet system which can be conﬁgured to build according to the need and application.DATACOM SX series is primarily catering Data centre Server, Storage, Networking need and it can easily be customized. The frame can be made either in Steel or in Aluminium & the static load capacity rating can reach up to 1500 kg, DATACOM SX Cabinet is a cleverly designed space-saving rack that oﬀers ample space for a wide range of equipment for mounting. The DATACOM SX supports a wide variety of equipment, including servers, storage, switches, routers, PDUs, UPSs, console port servers and KVM switches. This rack gives you the ﬂexibility you need with easy installation. DATACOM SX Cabinet size range from 22U to 47U with diﬀerent combination of width and depth.The front door is single-hinged and perforated. It features split perforated rear doors that open in the middle to minimize door swing footprint. This cabinet has two solid side panels. It includes casters, and two sets of cage nut equipment mounting rails. Al Jaddaf, Dubai, United Arab Emirates, P.O. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com HYBRID CABLE - POWER OVER FIBER FEATURESAPPLICATION DESCRIPTION DATACOM's innovative hybrid cables combine ﬁber optic or UTP data transmission with low-voltage power delivery in a single integrated solution. This breakthrough design eliminates the need for separate power and data cables, dramatically reducing installation complexity and cost. Hybrid composite ﬁber optic cables have both ﬁber and copper conductors under the same cable jacket to deliver data and power to the very edge of the enterprise network. DATACOM Hybrid is a Class 3-rated composite cable that supports low-voltage (Class 2, 57 VDC/100 V) as well as bulk power solutions. Hybrid cable can reach distances of over 2,000 feet, this cabling option is also ideal for long-reach or remote applications, such as security cameras in parking areas or outdoor, campus-wide Wi-F etc. • Power cable suitable for power networks. • Direct burial. • Outdoors • indoors and in cable ducts. • Conductor Diameter: 14 AWG, Class 2 Copper - (As per IEC 60228 Standard). • XLPE Insulated Core: 2.9 ± 0.1 mm overall Diam- eter (As per BS EN 60811-201 & BS EN 60811-501). • Operating Voltage Under Test Condition (100/250/500/1000 Mtr) : 56/120/230 VDC. • Operating Current Under Test Condition (100/250/500/1000 Mtr) : 1/2/5/10 Ampere • Conductor Resistance: 8.79 Ohm/Km • Conductors: Plain annealed stranded Copper • Insulation: Polyethylene (XLPE) as per BS 5467 standards. • Voltage: 600/1000V as per Standard IEC 60502 • Operating temperature: Maximum 90°C as per BS 5467 standards. • Core identiﬁcation: Two Core - Red & Black. name: High Density Vertical Cable Managers
searchText: CUSTOMIZED DATASHEETS/DATACOM OPEN RACK SOLUTION/DATACOM HIGH DENSITY VERTICAL CABLE MANAGERS.pdf High Density Vertical Cable Managers Al Jaddaf, Dubai, United Arab Emirates, P.O. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com DESCRIPTION Datacom High-Density Vertical Cable Managers are specially designed to meet the challenges of high-density cable management, driven by the increasing number of patch panels per rack and the introduction of angled jack panels. The cable manager frame is manufactured from high-quality steel sheet, which is punched, formed, welded, and powder-coated to ensure superior strength and durability. The doors are made from steel or aluminum sheet, also punched, formed, and powder-coated, meeting the highest quality standards to deliver a reliable and premium product. Standard conﬁgurations are available in 6”, 8”, 12”, and 15” widths. These units feature a unique double-hinged door on both the front and rear, along with removable and reusable ﬁngers made of durable polycarbonate materi- al. The product is available in both front-only and front-and-rear conﬁgurations to suit diﬀerent installation require- ments. FEATURES • Fingers can be removed very easily to suit site requirements. • Unique double-hinged door design at front and back allows the door to open from left to right or right to left for easy cable management. • The vertical cable manager is free-standing on the ﬂoor and can be bolted to open racks for better align ment and improved aesthetic appearance. • Composite construction: basic frame made of powder-coated steel and ﬁngers made of polycarbon ate for high strength to carry large quantities of cables. • Cable spools and slots are provided in the center support member for eﬃcient high-capacity cable management. • Polycarbonate ﬁngers ensure no cable damage during installation or maintenance. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com OPEN RACKS SEIRIES ( 2POST AND 4 POST) DESCRIPTION Datacom 4-Post Open Frame Racks are designed for high-density applications including networking, telephone and voice mail systems, routers, data centers, telecommunications, and laboratory environments.Manufactured from rugged aluminum/steel sheet proﬁles, the racks are powder-coated and precision-bolted to meet the highest quali- ty standards under the Datacom brand. The rigid construction combined with adjustable depth settings makes these racks ideal for supporting heavy and deep equipment that may exceed the capacity of standard 2-post bay racks • Conforms to DIN 41494 or equivalent ISO standards. • Top and bottom angles with heavy-duty assembly hardware enable the rack to handle heavier loads. • Ease of assembly with high-quality fasteners. • Numbered U positions. • High-strength, lightweight aluminum extrusion construction. • M6/12-24 cage nut mounting screws with combination Phillips/straight heads and pilot points to reduce cross-thread ing and installation time. • Panel mounting holes: M6/12-24 threads in both ﬂanges. • Conductive M6 tapped DIN standard holes ensure 100% conductivity with the rack, eliminating the need for a separate grounding strip. • Powder coating provides a uniform appearance and protec tion against aluminum oxidation corrosion. • Static load capacity of 1200 kg (weight must be evenly distrib uted). • Heavy-duty bottom (160×60×3Th) mm, top angles (40×40×3Th) mm, and verticals (32×76×32×5Th) mm ensure up to 1200 kg static load capacity. • Grounding and bonding options available. • Custom conﬁgurations and assemblies are available upon special order . name: INTELLIGENT ePATCH- (Epanel 24 Port COPPER)
searchText: CUSTOMIZED DATASHEETS/INTELLIGENT ePATCH/DATACOM INTELLIGENT ePATCH- (Epanel 24 Port COPPER).pdf INTELLIGENT ePATCH- (Epanel 24 Port COPPER) Al Jaddaf, Dubai, United Arab Emirates, P.O. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com INTELLIGENT ePATCH- (Epanel 24 Port COPPER) DESCRIPTION DATACOM 1U 24 ports shielded unloaded blank smart panel (e-panel) w/rear support, every port has dust-proof shutter . Every port has an RGB-led indicator to indicate the status of ports: •Green-authorized plugged. •Red-unauthorized unplugged. •Yellow-unauthorized plugged. There is a dual-RJ45-type control port at the rear side to connect with the Controller (Management unit) by Cat5e above standard patch cord. This epanel is a port-type-detect e-panel which can detect the status of the port if it is occupied or not. name: Intelligent Power Distribution Unit
searchText: PDU/SMART PDU/DATACOM INTELLIGENT POWER DISTRIBUTION UNIT-R00.pdf Intelligent Power Distribution Unit Al Jaddaf, Dubai, United Arab Emirates, P.O. Box: E: info@datacom-networking.com www.datacom-networking.com INTELLIGENT POWER DISTRIBUTION UNIT FEATURESAPPLICATION DESCRIPTION • Remote control of power for data center equipment. • Observe and obtain power event alerts for essential server and networking devices. • Generate energy schedules for manufac turing equipment. SENSORS (Optional) • Temperature & Humidity Sensor • Smoke Detector • Door Detector • Voltage (V), Current (A), Frequency (f), Active Power (kW), Real Power (kVA), Energy (kWh), Power Factor(PF) Metering. • Metering per PDU. • Metering per Branch Circuit Breaker. • Metering per Outlet. • Switched and Non Switched per Outlet. • Metering Accuracy +/- 1%. • SMTP; SNMP; Telnet; HTTPs; SSH; SMARTPack Management • Color graphic TFT display for any value and message displaying • RJ-45 (10/100 BaseT Ethernet) Network Connection • Cascading. • Supported Environmental Sensors are Temperature, Humidity, Air ﬂow, Water detection, Smoke detection, Door contact . Datacom Smart PDUs (Power Distribution Units) with remote monitoring capabilities play a crucial role in modern data centers by enabling real-time energy monitoring and intelligent power management. These intelligent PDUs provide granular insights into power consumption at the rack level, helping data center operators optimize energy usage, improve eﬃciency, and enhance capacity planning. DATACOM Smart PDU comes in four Series; • MAG Series. Basic Inline Monitoring PDUs provide aggregate power consumption data at the rack level, helping operators track total energy usage without per-outlet granularity. • MEO Series: Outlet-Monitoring PDUs oﬀer detailed, per-device power metrics, enabling precise energy allocation and identifying underutilized or overloaded equipment. • MAS Series :Switched PDUs add remote outlet-level control, allowing administrators to power cycle devices remotely, reducing downtime and improving maintenance eﬃciency. • MOS Series :Finally, Switched & Outlet-Monitoring PDUs combine both features, delivering real-time power analytics alongside remote power management—ideal for optimizing energy use, automating load shedding, and enhancing disaster recovery protocols. These Smart PDU models empower data centers to improve PUE (Power Usage Eﬀectiveness), streamline capacity planning, and ensure resilient power management in dynamic IT environments. SENSORS (Optional) • Temperature & Humidity Sensor • Smoke Detector • Door Detector • Voltage (V), Current (A), Frequency (f), Active Power (kW), Real Power (kVA), Energy (kWh), Power Factor(PF) Metering. • Metering per PDU. • Metering Accuracy +/- 1%. • SMTP; SNMP; Telnet; HTTPs; SSH; SMARTPack Management • Color graphic TFT display for any value and message displaying • RJ-45 (10/100 BaseT Ethernet) Network Connection • Supported Environmental Sensors are Temperature, Humidity, Air ﬂow, Water detection, Smoke detection, Door contact . Datacom Smart PDUs (Power Distribution Units) with remote monitoring capabilities play a crucial role in modern data centers by enabling real-time energy monitoring and intelligent power management. These intelligent PDUs provide granular insights into power consumption at the rack level, helping data center operators optimize energy usage, improve eﬃciency, and enhance capacity planning. DATACOM Smart PDU comes in four Series; • MAG Series. Basic Inline Monitoring PDUs provide aggregate power consumption data at the rack level, helping operators track total energy usage without per-outlet granularity. • MEO Series: Outlet-Monitoring PDUs oﬀer detailed, per-device power metrics, enabling precise energy allocation and identifying underutilized or overloaded equipment. • MAS Series :Switched PDUs add remote outlet-level control, allowing administrators to power cycle devices remotely, reducing downtime and improving maintenance eﬃciency. • MOS Series :Finally, Switched & Outlet-Monitoring PDUs combine both features, delivering real-time power analytics alongside remote power management—ideal for optimizing energy use, automating load shedding, and enhancing disaster recovery protocols. These Smart PDU models empower data centers to improve PUE (Power Usage Eﬀectiveness), streamline capacity planning, and ensure resilient power management in dynamic IT environments. name: Smart PDU Installation Dodument
searchText: PDU/SMART PDU/SMART PDU INSTALLATION DODUMENT.pdf Smart PDU Installation Dodument  /gid00010/gid00041/gid00047/gid00032/gid00039/gid00039/gid00036/gid00034/gid00032/gid00041/gid00047/gid00001/gid00017/gid00042/gid00050/gid00032/gid00045/gid00001/gid00014/gid00028/gid00041/gid00028/gid00034/gid00032/gid00040/gid00032/gid00041/gid00047 Table of Contents 01 Introduction 02 Product Overview 03 Product Series 04 Key Features 05 Controller Panel Components 06 PDU Cascade Configuration 07 Contact Information Introduction DATACOM Smart PDUs (Power Distribution Units) with remote monitoring capabilities play a crucial role in modern data centers by enabling real-time energy monitoring and intelligent power management. These intelligent PDUs provide granular insights into power consumption at the rack level, helping data center operators optimize energy usage, improve efficiency, and enhance capacity planning. These Smart PDU models empower data centers to improve PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness), streamline capacity planning, and ensure resilient power management in dynamic IT environments. Key Features Advanced Intelligent Capabilities The DATACOM Smart PDU series provides administrators with a comprehensive, secure, and reliable power management solution for data centers and server rooms. 01 Remote Management The smart PDU allows users to remotely manage and control the power supply of each socket through the network. Administrators can monitor and control the power status of devices in real- time from any location with network connectivity, without physically accessing the device location. 02 Electricity Monitoring Equipped with advanced electricity monitoring functions that track the electricity consumption of each socket in real time. Administrators can view power utilization, power factors, and current load data to assist in energy management and optimization. 03 Environmental Monitoring Features environmental monitoring capabilities to track temperature and humidity in computer rooms or data centers. This helps administrators promptly identify and resolve potential environmental issues to protect safe device operation. 04 Timed Scheduling Users can configure scheduled operations to automatically control socket opening and closing times. This feature supports regular equipment maintenance, energy conservation, and coordinated equipment usage timing. 05 Alarm and Notification Generates real-time alarms and notifications when abnormal situations are detected, such as current overload, temperature abnormalities, or device disconnection. Administrators receive immediate notifications to take timely corrective measures. 06 Security Designed with multiple security mechanisms including user authentication, access control, and encrypted communication to ensure management permission security and prevent unauthorized access or potential security risks. PDU Cascade Configuration Scalable Network Architecture The DATACOM Smart PDU supports advanced cascading capabilities to optimize network infrastructure and reduce costs. Cascade Capacity Up to 99 PDUs can be cascaded together using standard RJ45 patch cords, significantly reducing the need for expensive network ports. Master-Slave Architecture The master PDU's embedded web interface provides centralized control: Lists all connected slave PDUs Monitors electrical parameters across all units Controls individual outlet switching Provides unified management dashboard Configuration Limitations While the master PDU can control and monitor slave units, IP address configuration for slave PDUs cannot be changed through the master interface. Recommended Setup For large-scale installations and simplified management, DATACOM recommends deploying central monitoring software with a dedicated server device. This approach provides: Streamlined mass installation Enhanced management capabilities Centralized configuration control Comprehensive reporting and analytics Controller Panel Components Main Interface Elements The Smart PDU controller panel features an intuitive interface with the following key components: 01 LCD Liquid Crystal Display Screen Provides real-time visual feedback on power metrics, system status, and configuration settings. 02 Navigation Keys UP: Page up navigation DOWN: Page down navigation FUN: Settings and configuration key 03 Indicator Lights Long-distance indicator lights Alarm indicator lights for immediate visual alerts 04 DI Interface Sensor interface for connecting environmental monitoring sensors. 05 485 Interface 485 sensor interface for additional monitoring capabilities. 06 485A/485B 485 cascade interface for connecting multiple PDUs in a daisy-chain configuration. 07 NET Network Interface Gigabit network port
